news.title: Chandigarh wants to rise higher but can its roads take the load?
news.para: Chandigarh's ambitious development plans, including higher building limits and mixed land use, are raising serious concerns about traffic congestion.  Official reports and resident objections highlight a critical gap: the city is densifying without a clear plan for its overloaded transport infrastructure.  Decades-old warnings about saturated roads and a lack of mass transit are now a stark reality, with private vehicles dominating and speeds plummeting.

news.title: Ludhiana: LIT slashes reserve price to ₹182 crore in fresh bid to auction its commercial building
news.para: Ludhiana Improvement Trust has slashed the reserve price of a prime commercial building by ₹15 crore to ₹182 crore in a fresh e-auction, hoping to attract buyers after previous attempts failed. The 18-year-old, abandoned property, featuring extensive commercial space and parking, has suffered from neglect. LIT is also considering price cuts for other properties due to poor market response.

news.title: Manesar civic body razes six concrete plants, 30 more under scanner
news.para: Authorities in Gurugram have intensified their crackdown on illegal operations, demolishing six ready-mix concrete plants on Garhi-Basai Road for lacking mandatory permissions. This action follows a similar demolition last week. Despite these efforts, over 30 unauthorized plants remain operational, raising concerns about regulatory oversight. Officials vow to take action against all remaining illegal establishments, urging businesses to secure necessary approvals before commencing operations.

news.title: Varanasi: VDA intensifies infrastructure development at three proposed townships
news.para: Varanasi Development Authority is set to launch plot registrations for its flagship Anand Kashi Yojana by August end, marking a significant township project after 32 years. The 250-acre development, inspired by Kashi's heritage, will feature extensive infrastructure, including roads, green spaces, and civic amenities.  This project, along with Rudra Vihar and Sports City, is receiving substantial government backing and is closely monitored by the Chief Minister.

news.title: Navi Mumbai civic body nets ₹434 crore in property tax in Q1 FY27
news.para: Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation has achieved a record Rs 434.02 crore in property tax collection for the first quarter of 2026-27, a significant rise from the previous year. This success is attributed to technology-driven administration, data analytics, and enhanced digital payment options, with over 72% of taxes paid online. The initiative also recovered dues from chronic defaulters, showcasing citizen trust and contribution to city development.

news.title: Haryana caps daily property registration appointments at 20 per office to ensure faster services
news.para: Haryana has introduced a new token system for property registrations, limiting daily appointments to 20 per tehsil and sub-divisional office. This random allocation aims to prevent favoritism and speed up the process for citizens. The move is expected to enhance transparency, reduce waiting times, and curb the influence of middlemen, making property dealings more efficient and citizen-friendly across the state.

news.title: Housing affordability steady in six of eight cities in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Ahmedabad remained India’s most affordable housing market with an affordability ratio of 23%, followed by Kolkata at 25% and Pune at 28%, according to Knight Frank India. MMR and NCR continued to exceed the affordability threshold of 50%.

news.title: DTCP resumes demolition drive in Gurugram DLF phase-III
news.para: Authorities in Gurgaon have intensified their crackdown on illegal constructions and paying guest accommodations in DLF Phase 3, following High Court directives.  The drive saw the demolition of unauthorized structures in parking areas, sealing of PG buildings, and removal of encroachments from public land.  Action was taken uniformly, even at actor Chandrachur Singh's residence, with further operations planned.

news.title: Four illegal commercial buildings demolished in Ludhiana
news.para: Ludhiana's municipal corporation has intensified its drive against illegal constructions, demolishing four under-construction buildings across Zone D on Friday.  These structures were erected in clear defiance of building bylaws, even after official notices were issued.  Authorities are urging residents and developers to secure approved plans before commencing any construction to prevent similar enforcement actions.

news.title: Arihant Foundations acquires land parcel in Chennai for ₹300 crore office project
news.para: Arihant Foundations & Housing has acquired over half an acre on Anna Salai, adjacent to Hyatt Regency Chennai. The company plans to develop a boutique Grade-A commercial office project with an estimated GDV of ₹300 crore.

news.title: Prime minister to lay foundation stone for Jaipur metro phase-II on July 4
news.para: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is inaugurating Phase II of the Jaipur Metro, a five-year project set to revolutionize connectivity along Tonk Road. This expansion includes a significant elevated stretch from Sitapura to Pinjrapole and an underground section near the airport.  Traffic management plans are in place to minimize disruption, with a focus on creating a 'Model Corridor' to ensure smooth commutes during construction.

news.title: No misappropriation of flats, TDR in Kothrud SRA scheme: Maharashtra minister
news.para: State minister Shambhuraj Desai has denied any misappropriation of flats or fraudulent transfer of development rights in Pune's Kothrud slum rehabilitation project. Responding to allegations of fake beneficiaries and financial loss, Desai stated due process was followed. The urban development department will review submitted documents and hold a meeting to address the concerns raised by MLA Sunil Kamble and other elected members regarding SRA schemes.

news.title: Aadhaar e-KYC, QR code a must for property registration in Haryana
news.para: Haryana has revolutionized property registration with a new tech-driven system. Aadhaar e-KYC, biometrics, and digital signatures are now mandatory, enhancing security and transparency. Registered documents will feature QR codes for instant verification, combating fraud. The process is streamlined, eliminating physical NOCs and allowing online tracking, document uploads, and even Tatkal upgrades for urgent needs. This initiative aims to save time and money for citizens while curbing fraudulent practices.

news.title: NCDRC orders Pune builder to refund ₹7 crore for project on government land
news.para: A real estate firm and its owner must refund over Rs7 crore to 113 homebuyers after a 14-year legal battle. The National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission found the developer sold properties on land acquired by the government in 1978 for a public project.

news.title: Odisha RERA revises QPR format for real estate projects
news.para: Odisha Real Estate Regulatory Authority (ORERA) has revised its quarterly progress report format for real estate projects, specifically rectifying errors in the financial status pro forma. The updated format, effective from June 2026, demands clearer details on fund collection, transfers to the RERA account, and expenditures from various sources. This aims to enhance transparency and simplify tracking of project finances for homebuyers and the regulator.

news.title: Landowners equally liable with builders for incomplete housing projects: Kerala-RERA
news.para: Landowners in Kerala are now equally liable with builders for incomplete housing projects if they are financially involved. The Kerala Real Estate Regulatory Authority (K-RERA) ruled that landowners actively participating in finances or profit-sharing will be treated as 'joint promoters'. This landmark decision follows a case where a landowner mortgaged project land for a loan, defaulting and impacting homebuyers.

news.title: RBI imposes ₹66.7 lakh penalty on Bank of Baroda, GIC Housing Finance
news.para: The Reserve Bank of India has levied significant penalties on Bank of Baroda and GIC Housing Finance for regulatory breaches. Bank of Baroda faces a Rs 63.6 lakh fine for charging excess interest and delayed KYC record uploads. GIC Housing Finance has been fined Rs 3.1 lakh for failing to conduct timely risk reviews of accounts. These actions highlight the RBI's commitment to enforcing fair lending and customer identification practices.

news.title: Sobha records sales value of ₹3,656 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Sobha achieved sales value of ₹3,656 crore in Q1 FY27, up 76% year-on-year, with 2.34 million sq ft of area sold across 1,432 homes and plots. The company launched 6.89 million sq ft of saleable area across three projects in Bengaluru and Gurugram during the quarter.
